trigger: Use when a manifest diff changes a dependency version and the new or old version may carry a published CVE.
context_plan: Inspect package.json / requirements.txt / pyproject.toml for the old and new pinned versions
Look up both versions in OSV/GHSA
Flag CVEs introduced by the upgrade AND CVEs fixed by it
checklist: Name the specific CVE / GHSA / OSV id when one applies
State whether each CVE is introduced or fixed by the change
Give the fixed-in version if available
test_templates: Pin the dependency to the fixed-in version in the lockfile and assert it via a CI lint
Add a smoke test that fails if the vulnerable code path is exercised

trigger: Use when a manifest diff bumps a dependency's major version, indicating an API or behavior break per semver.
context_plan: Compare old and new major versions against semver semantics
Identify which call sites in the repo use the upgraded package
Cross-reference the CHANGELOG, MIGRATION, or BREAKING.md if available
checklist: List concrete API changes that this code base touches
Note default-behavior changes (timeouts, encoding, retries) that may silently change runtime behavior
test_templates: Run the existing test suite against the new version in a feature branch
Add a contract test for the deprecated symbol path

trigger: Use when a manifest diff adds, removes, or upgrades a dependency that may pull in or drop transitive packages.
risk_profile: New transitive in the supply-chain attack surface
context_plan: Resolve the dependency tree before and after the change
Look up new transitives in OSV for known compromises (e.g. event-stream 3.3.6)
checklist: List newly introduced transitive packages by name
Flag any transitive with maintainer/scope changes
Recommend resolutions or overrides when a known-bad transitive is being pulled in
test_templates: Run npm audit / pip-audit and gate CI on the result
Snapshot the lockfile and diff transitives on every PR

trigger: Use when a manifest diff changes a dependency in a way that may change the cumulative license obligations of the project.
context_plan: Look up the SPDX license of each changed dependency before and after
checklist: Name the SPDX id of the new and old licenses
Recommend legal review when a non-permissive license appears for the first time
test_templates: Add a CI check using `pip-licenses` or `license-checker` that fails on unknown / forbidden SPDX ids

trigger: Use when a manifest diff uses or changes a version specifier (^, ~, *, range, exact, or git tag).
risk_profile: Floating range (^X.Y.Z, ~X.Y) in a production manifest
Lockfile missing or stale relative to manifest
context_plan: Read the manifest and any sibling lockfile
Categorize each pin as exact, caret, tilde, wildcard, or git ref
Flag wildcards and unpinned specifiers in production
checklist: Recommend exact pinning + lockfile for reproducible builds
test_templates: CI step that fails when manifest is out of sync with lockfile
Reproducible-build test: install from lockfile and diff resolved versions

trigger: Use when a manifest diff adds or retains a dependency that may be deprecated, abandoned, or marked as such on its registry.
context_plan: Check registry deprecation flags (npm `deprecated` field, PyPI `Development Status`)
Note replacement packages where the registry suggests them
Flag abandoned packages without a clear successor
checklist: Name the package, the deprecation status, and the suggested replacement
Flag continued use of unmaintained dependencies in a security-sensitive surface
Propose a migration path with effort estimate
test_templates: CI check that fails when a deprecated package appears in the lockfile
